title: Artanthe lineata
intro: The Artanthe lineata, also known as the Artanthe vine or Houttuynia cordata var. lineata, is a type of flowering plant in the family Saururaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Artanthe lineata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Artanthe lineata is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Artanthe lineata is a species of plant in the family Araliaceae. It is native to tropical Asia. The classification of Artanthe lineata is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the class Magnoliopsida. This plant is also known as the 'Asian Artanthe' and is commonly found in the wild. The exact classification of Artanthe lineata can be broken down into its binomial nomenclature.
